page_content:
ENST640 Advanced Crops, Soils, and Civilization (3 Credits) Explore the rise, cycling, resilience, and decline of civilizations and apply learning to modern issues in the ﬁelds of sustainability, environmental ethics, natural resource management, and agriculture. Role and importance of crop and soil resources in the development of human civilization. History of crop and soil use and management as they relate to the persistence of ancient and modern cultures.Jointly offered with ENST440. Credit Only Granted for: ENST440 or ENST6 40.

ENST643 Advanced Industrial Ecology (3 Credits) Problems of waste management and recycling in human societies are covered. The industrial ecology approach to design is contrasted with analogous patterns and processes from natural ecosystems. Prerequisite: BSCI361; and permission of AGNR-Environmental Science & Technology department. Jointly offered with: ENST443. Credit Only Granted for: ENST443 or ENST643.
